free_text: Quasi-stellar neutrons from the Li-7(p,n)Be-7 reaction
with an energy-broadened proton beam.
prelim.results
7-MV Van de Graaff accelerator at IRMM
Schematic of the 6Li-glass detector -
aluminum can-0.6-mm thick, Si rubber-1 mm,
Teflon layer- 0.25 mm,  6Li glass- 25.4 mm
The stationary 6Li-glass detector was used to
monitor beam stability and for normalization.
LiF targets of 6-mm diameter were prepared by
evaporation on a 1-mm thick Cu disk, 40.7 mm in
diameter. The LiF layer thickness was determined by
weighing to be in the range of 1 to 2 mg/cm**2. A
gold foil nominally 2-mum thick was used as an energy
degrader. The foil was glued on a copper ring placing
it 1 mm upstream of the LiF layer to minimize
transverse beam growth by the scattering in the gold
foil.
Authors use the term "broad-energy proton beam" for
protons that impinge on the LiF target after a gold
degrader foil in case of an incident energy of
2096.5 keV before the degrader and use the term
"narrow-energy proton beam" for a proton beam of
1912 keV directly from the Van de Graaff accelerator.
No information about source of uncertainty
Table data were received from Dr.G.Feinberg
S.B.
SD: A few parameters were added to
Subents 002-005.
broad-energy proton beam
Ep=1912+/-1.5 keV data
SD: ANG-ERR, EN-RSL, EN-ERR were added.
En=2096.5 -> 1913.keV. SF6=DA/DE -> MLT/DA/DE,
SF8=REL -> TT/REL in REACTION code.
broad-energy proton beam
Ep=1912+/-1.5 keV data
SD: EN-RSL, EN-ERR were added. En=2096.5 ->
1913.keV.  SF6=DE -> MLT/DE, SF8=REL -> TT/REL in
REACTION code.
Narrow-energy proton beam
Ep=1913+/-21 keV data
SD: ANG-ERR, EN-RSL were added. SF6=DA/DE->
MLT/DA/DE, SF8=REL -> TT/REL in REACTION code.
